title: ble.rs: AppFrame silently drops payloads exceeding 512 bytes
description:
file: rust/lichen-kiss/src/ble.rs
lines: 115-123

When frame.data exceeds the 512-byte heapless::Vec capacity, extend_from_slice fails and the frame is silently dropped:

```rust
let mut data: heapless::Vec<u8, 512> = heapless::Vec::new();
if data.extend_from_slice(frame.data).is_err() {
    #[cfg(feature = "log")]
    warn!("kiss_ble: try_get_app_frame payload too large ({} bytes)", frame.data.len());
    return None;
}
```

Problems:
1. Returns None (indistinguishable from no-data-available)
2. Warning only logged if 'log' feature is enabled
3. No way for caller to know a valid frame was received but dropped

Related to project-LICHEN-z2m3 (cannot distinguish no-data from parse-error) but this is specifically about oversized payload truncation.

Impact: Large frames silently dropped, caller cannot distinguish from empty queue.

Fix: Return Result<Option<AppFrame>, Error> to surface capacity errors, or increase Vec capacity.
